name: Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop
name_original: Desfiladeros del Río Ebro — circular desde Orbaneja del Castillo
description: Hike the moderate 10.5-mile Ebro Canyon Trail loop from Orbaneja del Castillo, featuring waterfalls, canyon views, and unique rock formation
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Sedano offers diverse landscapes, from the impressive Ebro Canyon with its waterfalls and rock formations to vast páramos and lomas. The region features a fascinating karstic landscape, characterized by a labyrinth of rocks, water, and greenery. Lush forests and various valleys, such as the Valles Románicos, contribute to the area's rich flora and fauna, all set within the mountainous terrain of Burgos. Best hiking trails around Valle De Sedano The most popular hiking route is Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the dramatic Ebro Canyon and passes through the village of Orbaneja del Castillo. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Orbaneja del Castillo – View of Orbaneja del Castillo loop from Castillo de Orbaneja, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This trail offers scenic views of Orbaneja del Castillo and its surroundings. Local hikers also love the Orbaneja del Castillo – View of Orbaneja del Castillo loop from Quintanilla-Escalada, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail leading through the picturesque area around Orbaneja del Castillo, often completed in about 5 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Sedano is defined by the Ebro Canyon, karstic landscapes, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Valle De Sedano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Nearly 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Sedano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Sedano?
A: Valle De Sedano offers a diverse network of 17 hiking trails. These routes explore the region's unique landscapes, from the dramatic Ebro Canyon to tranquil páramos and lush forests.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hikes in Valle De Sedano?
A: The trails in Valle De Sedano cater to various fitness levels. There are 6 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging hikes, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.

Q: What makes hiking in Valle De Sedano unique?
A: Hiking in Valle De Sedano is unique due to its contrasting landscapes. You'll encounter the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Cañón del Ebro&lt;/strong&gt; with its waterfalls and rock formations, vast &lt;em&gt;páramos&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;loras&lt;/em&gt;, and a fascinating karstic landscape. The region also boasts ancient dolmens and medieval villages, offering a rich blend of natural beauty and historical intrigue.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valle De Sedano has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Orbaneja del Castillo – View of Orbaneja del Castillo loop from Castillo de Orbaneja&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.7 km) path offering scenic views of the charming village of Orbaneja del Castillo.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Valle De Sedano?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its beautiful water features. You can explore trails that lead to the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;, where water flows directly through the village. Other notable waterfalls include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tubilla del Agua Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valle De Sedano?
A: Many of the trails in Valle De Sedano are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR99 – Escalada Viewpoint loop from Castillo de Orbaneja&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.8 miles (12.6 km) and offers diverse scenery.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while hiking?
A: Valle De Sedano is rich in history. Hiking trails often pass through enchanting medieval villages and monumental enclaves showcasing Romanesque art. The region is also home to one of Europe's largest collections of dolmens and megalithic sites, with over 30 cataloged funerary monuments. You can also find archaeological sites like a high-medieval necropolis in El Castro.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are challenging routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orbaneja del Castillo – View of Orbaneja del Castillo loop from Quintanilla-Escalada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.8-mile (17.4 km) trail that takes approximately 5 hours to complete, offering a more demanding experience through the picturesque area around Orbaneja del Castillo.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Valle De Sedano?
A: The trails in Valle De Sedano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic canyon views, the tranquility of the páramos, and the unique natural features like the waterfalls flowing through Orbaneja del Castillo.

Q: Are there any castles or historical viewpoints to visit?
A: Yes, the region features several historical sites and viewpoints. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbel Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Loja&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic vistas, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4967654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Castillo de los Rojas&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the area's past and stunning panoramas.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The landscapes in Valle De Sedano are incredibly diverse. You'll traverse the deep gorges of the Ebro Canyon, vast high plains known as &lt;em&gt;páramos&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;loras&lt;/em&gt;, and lush forests. The area's karstic nature means you'll also encounter unique rock formations, caves, and abundant springs, creating a constantly changing and captivating environment.
tips: Beautiful path that goes in the direction of Villaescusa de Ebro and is close to the Ebro river. The naturalized space meanders along the meanders and gorges offered by the river itself in this depressed area that the waters have created over the years.
This beautiful waterfall that flows into the Ebro River has its origins in a karstic natural spring and is surrounded by a wonderful environment that is undoubtedly worth visiting.
In this small area called Estilla, there are a group of small stone cabins that had been shelters for shepherds and farmers.
Stunning views from any point on the PR above Orbaneja.
It is beautiful on its own, but if we add the depths of the town from which it arises... visit it!

faqs.question: Where does the Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends in the picturesque village of Orbaneja del Castillo. Parking is available within or near the village, though it can get busy, especially during peak season. Arriving early is recommended to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hiking route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse terrain, including rocky ledges, cobbled tracks, and paths winding through forests and farmlands. The route also ascends to high plateaus, offering varied walking surfaces. Sure-footedness is advised, especially on steeper sections.

faqs.question: What unique natural and historical features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular views of the Ebro Canyon and the Ebro River. You'll encounter unique rock formations like "El Castillo" and the "Kiss of the Camels." Don't miss the stunning Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo flowing through the village, and the Tobazo Waterfall. You might also spot ancient Stone Huts of Estilla on the plateaus, remnants of pastoral life.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While the full 16.9 km loop is rated as moderate and requires good fitness and sure-footedness due to some steep sections, shorter, easier loops around Orbaneja del Castillo are available and suitable for various fitness levels. This specific loop is best for those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially on exposed sections, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions on higher ground.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it passes through the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife, especially the rich birdlife including griffon vultures, and to respect other hikers and local regulations. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike this trail, nor is there an entrance fee. The trail is freely accessible as it is located within the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, which does not charge for entry. However, always respect park regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and follows sections of several other paths, including the Camino natural de Ebro - Castilla y León, Camino Natural del Ebro, and Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria. You'll also find parts of the Cañón del Ebro and Camino de Espinosa de Bricia a Orbaneja del Castillo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants in Orbaneja del Castillo?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Orbaneja del Castillo, being a popular tourist destination, offers a few cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. It's a great place to enjoy local cuisine and refreshments.

faqs.question: What is the best strategy for experiencing the village and the trail without crowds?
faqs.answer: To best enjoy both the village and the trail, consider starting your hike early in the morning, especially on weekends or during peak tourist seasons. This allows you to find parking more easily, experience the village's charm before it gets crowded, and enjoy the trail in cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Ebro Canyon?
faqs.answer: The dramatic canyon section is known for its rich birdlife. Keep an eye out for a large colony of griffon vultures soaring overhead. You might also spot other raptors and various smaller bird species, as well as typical Iberian wildlife in the surrounding forests.

waypoints.name: Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo
